varieties.name: Dwarf Cashew (Generic)
varieties.origin: Various dwarf selections from Brazil and India, 1980s-2000s
varieties.traits: Compact growth 4-6m height (vs 12-14m for standard), earlier bearing (2-3 years vs 5-6 years), spreading canopy suitable for containers
varieties.yield_notes: Higher yields per tree than standard types (1-2 tonnes/ha vs 0.25 tonnes/ha), more productive for space
varieties.availability: Daleys Fruit Tree Nursery, Ross Creek Tropicals, Fruitopia Nursery (Brisbane/Gold Coast), online tropical plant suppliers. Usually sold as seedlings or air-layers, occasionally grafted.

varieties.name: Red Dwarf Cashew
varieties.origin: Selected dwarf cultivar, likely Brazilian or Indian origin
varieties.traits: Compact 4-5m, distinctive red cashew apples, ornamental value, dense branching
varieties.availability: Specialty tropical fruit nurseries, limited availability in Brisbane region

varieties.name: Yellow Dwarf Cashew
varieties.origin: Selected dwarf cultivar
varieties.traits: Compact growth 4-5m, yellow cashew apples, similar management to Red Dwarf

varieties.name: Seedling Cashew (Unselected)
varieties.origin: Various, grown from imported or local seeds
varieties.traits: Highly variable - may grow 8-14m tall, branching and canopy structure unpredictable, slow to bear (5-8 years), wide genetic variability
varieties.yield_notes: Unpredictable and usually low (2-10 kg/tree), quality highly variable

varieties.name: Semecarpus australiensis 🌏 (Native Australian Cashew)
varieties.origin: Native to northern Queensland and Northern Territory coastal rainforests; related to but distinct from Anacardium
varieties.traits: Large rainforest tree up to 40m, straight cylindrical trunk with lenticels, produces tar-like black exudate when damaged, glossy dark green leaves 30cm long
varieties.availability: Not commercially available; wild populations in natural habitat only

varieties.name: KAU Nihara 🌱 (Dwarf)
varieties.origin: Selection from local germplasm in Thaliparamba, Kannur. Released 2019 by CRS Madakkathara, KAU
varieties.traits: FIRST OFFICIALLY RELEASED DWARF VARIETY IN INDIA. Unique compact canopy 3-4m height, manageable structure ideal for high-density planting, urban/peri-urban households
varieties.yield_notes: 1.25 tonnes/ha despite small size. Suitable for home gardens, rooftop cultivation, small-scale intensive farming
varieties.availability: KAU Cashew Research Station Madakkathara, Kerala nurseries, specialized tropical fruit suppliers

varieties.name: Amrutha (H-1597)
varieties.origin: Hybrid BLA-139-1 × H-3-13. Released 1998 by CRS Madakkathara, KAU
varieties.traits: Spreading canopy, extensive branching, mid-season flowering (December-January), fruiting January-March

varieties.name: Akshaya (H-7-6)
varieties.origin: Hybrid H-4-7 × K-30-1. Released 1998 by CRS Anakkayam, Malappuram, KAU
varieties.traits: Compact canopy, intensive branching ideal for high-density planting, mid-season flowering (December-January)
varieties.yield_notes: 11.78 kg/tree, premium quality kernels command highest prices
varieties.availability: CRS Madakkathara and Anakkayam, Kerala nurseries

varieties.name: Anagha (H-8-1)
varieties.origin: Hybrid T20 × K-30-1. Released 1998 by CRS Anakkayam, Malappuram, KAU
varieties.traits: Compact canopy, intensive branching, mid-season flowering (January-February), fruiting February-April
varieties.yield_notes: 13.73 kg/tree average, reliable producer
varieties.availability: CRS Madakkathara and Anakkayam, Kerala nurseries

varieties.name: Sree
varieties.origin: Released 2019 by CRS Madakkathara, KAU. Developed specifically for Kerala agro-climatic conditions

varieties.name: Mrudula (PTR1) 🌱
varieties.origin: Selection from Pattannur region, Kannur district, Kerala. Indigenous Kerala germplasm
varieties.traits: LATE-SEASON variety, UNIQUE FEATURE: absence of cashew nut shell liquid (CNSL) - rare characteristic making processing easier and safer

varieties.name: Poornima
varieties.origin: Hybrid BLA 139-1 × K-30-1. Released 2006, KAU
varieties.traits: Compact canopy, intensive branching, well-suited for dense planting and organized orchard management
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity in high-density systems

varieties.name: Dhana (H-1608)
varieties.origin: Hybrid variety, KAU release
varieties.traits: Mid-season variety, good branching, suitable for standard spacing
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to good yields, reliable producer

varieties.name: Dharasree (H-3-17)
varieties.origin: Hybrid variety, KAU release
varieties.traits: Good spreading growth, suitable for standard planting systems
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate yields, stable producer in Kerala conditions

varieties.name: K-22-1 🌱
varieties.origin: Selection from Tree No. 22, released 1987 by CRS Madakkathara, KAU. One of earliest KAU varieties
varieties.traits: Early flowering (October-November), fruiting December-January - EARLIEST variety under cultivation. Pinkish-yellow apples, 71% juice content
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate yields but EARLY HARVEST advantage

varieties.name: Kanaka (H-1598)
varieties.origin: Hybrid variety, KAU
varieties.traits: Mid-season flowering and fruiting, good canopy structure
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity in Kerala conditions

varieties.name: Madakkathara-1 (BLA-39-4) 🌱
varieties.origin: Selection from Bapatla germplasm (Tree 39), released 1985. One of FIRST VARIETIES released at national level based on multilocation trials
varieties.traits: Short flowering duration (November-January), early variety. Spreading canopy, extensive branching
varieties.yield_notes: 13.8 kg/tree average

varieties.name: Madakkathara-2 (NDR-2-1)
varieties.origin: Selection from Neduvellur germplasm collection, released 1987 by CRS Madakkathara
varieties.traits: Mid-season variety, good branching structure
varieties.yield_notes: 17 kg/tree - good yielder

varieties.name: Priyanka (H-1591)
varieties.origin: Hybrid variety, KAU
varieties.traits: Compact canopy, good branching for organized orchards
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity in Kerala climate

varieties.name: Sulabha (K-10-2)
varieties.origin: Selection variety, released 1996 by CRS Madakkathara, KAU
varieties.traits: Compact canopy, intensive branching, mid-season flowering (January-February), fruiting February-April
varieties.yield_notes: EXCELLENT 21.9 kg/tree average - one of highest yielding selections

varieties.name: Anakkayam-1 (BLA-139-1) 🌱
varieties.origin: Selection from Bapatla germplasm collection, released 1985. One of OLDEST released varieties (40 years)
varieties.traits: Spreading canopy, extensive branching. Short flowering duration.

varieties.name: Vengurla-4 🌱 🏆
varieties.origin: Selection from Regional Fruit Research Station, Vengurla, Maharashtra. Released 1981. First cashew with GEOGRAPHICAL INDICATION TAG (2016)
varieties.traits: Compact growth, early bearing, RED APPLE variety with bold nuts. Flowering January-March, harvest end January-March
varieties.yield_notes: Good to high yields, reliable producer

varieties.name: Vengurla-1 🌱 🏆
varieties.origin: Selection from Ansur village germplasm, Vengurla Tehsil. Released 1974. OLDEST Vengurla variety (51 years)
varieties.traits: EARLY FLOWERING variety, 8% perfect flowers, short flowering/fruiting phase
varieties.yield_notes: 19 kg/tree average - very good yields

varieties.name: Vengurla-2 🌱 🏆
varieties.origin: Selection from West Bengal germplasm (WBDC VI), released 1979
varieties.traits: 8% perfect flowers, short flowering/fruiting phase concentrating harvest
varieties.yield_notes: 24 kg/tree (20th year) - HIGHEST yielding Vengurla variety

varieties.name: Vengurla-7 🧬
varieties.origin: Hybrid Vengurla-3 × M 10/4 (VRI-1), Maharashtra-Tamil Nadu cross
varieties.traits: VERY HIGH perfect flower percentage (40% - exceptional), bold nut type
varieties.yield_notes: 18.5 kg/tree average - high yields
varieties.availability: RFRS Vengurla, Goa and Kerala nurseries, widely planted

varieties.name: Ullal-1 🌱
varieties.origin: Selection from Agricultural Research Station, Ullal, Karnataka. Released for entire Karnataka, widely adopted in Kerala
varieties.traits: EARLY BEARING cultivar with high sex ratio, intensive branching. Flowering late November-mid March, harvest complete by May (ESCAPES MONSOON)
varieties.yield_notes: 19.6 kg/tree - high yielding

varieties.name: Ullal-2 🌱
varieties.origin: Selection from ARS Ullal, Karnataka. Released for entire Karnataka and HIGHLY CONGENIAL FOR HILL ZONES
varieties.traits: Early flowering/bearing, high sex ratio, HIGH SHELLING 30%
varieties.yield_notes: 17.9 kg/tree - very good yields

varieties.name: Ullal-3
varieties.origin: Selection from ARS Ullal, Karnataka
varieties.traits: Flowering November-January (60-70 days duration), harvest January-March (60-90 days)
varieties.yield_notes: 14.68 kg/tree (10th year) - moderate to good yields

varieties.name: Ullal-4
varieties.origin: Selection from Tuni, Andhra Pradesh material. Released 1994 by ARS Ullal
varieties.traits: Open canopy, intensive branching, SHORT-DURATION cropping cycle. Flowering November-January, fruiting January-March
varieties.yield_notes: 9.5 kg/tree average (moderate), but EARLY MATURITY and high quality compensate

varieties.name: Goa-1 🌱
varieties.origin: Selection from Balli-2 accession, Balli village, Quepem taluk, Goa. Released 1999 by ICAR Research Complex, Goa. FIRST VARIETY RELEASED FROM GOA STATE
varieties.traits: Mid-season variety, adapted to coastal laterite soils and high humidity
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to good yields, reliable in coastal conditions

varieties.name: Goa-2 🌱
varieties.origin: Selection from Ela village, Tiswadi taluk, North Goa. Released 2007 by ICAR Research Complex, Goa
varieties.traits: Mid-season variety
varieties.yield_notes: 5.5 kg/tree average (moderate but consistent)
varieties.availability: ICAR Research Complex Goa, limited Kerala availability

varieties.name: Bhaskara (Goa 11/6) 🌱
varieties.origin: Selection from Goa germplasm, ICAR Research Complex, Goa
varieties.traits: Compact canopy suitable for high-density planting
varieties.yield_notes: RESPONDS WELL TO HIGH-DENSITY PLANTING - Normal planting (8×8m): 737.88 kg/ha; HIGH-DENSITY (5×5m): 1882.54 kg/ha (2.5× increase)
varieties.availability: ICAR Research Complex Goa, Karnataka CDC, some Kerala nurseries

varieties.name: VRI (CW) H1
varieties.origin: Hybrid released 2009 by Regional Research Station, Vridhachalam, TNAU
varieties.traits: ESPECIALLY SUITABLE FOR RAIN-FED cultivation - bred for low-input systems
varieties.yield_notes: IMPRESSIVE 2900 kg/ha (exceptionally high for rain-fed conditions)

varieties.name: Vridhachalam-3 (M 26/2) 🌱
varieties.origin: Selection from Edayanchavadi material, released 1991 by RRS Vridhachalam
varieties.traits: Compact canopy, intensive branching, EARLY FLOWERING (January-February), fruiting February-May
varieties.yield_notes: 11.68 kg/tree - good yields, early harvest advantage

varieties.name: BPP-8 (H2/16)
varieties.origin: Selection from Cashew Research Station, Bapatla, Andhra Pradesh
varieties.traits: Compact canopy, suitable for organized plantations. Identified as suitable variety for RAINFED cultivation in coastal Andhra Pradesh
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields under rainfed conditions, reliable producer

varieties.name: Local Seedling Types (Nadan/Traditional) 🌱 🌏
varieties.origin: Indigenous Kerala cashews, descended from Portuguese introductions 1560s-1600s. 400+ years natural selection in Kerala conditions
varieties.traits: HIGHLY VARIABLE - each tree genetically unique. Typically tall (10-14m), late bearing (5-8 years), irregular yields. Found in old homesteads, temple groves, traditional farms throughout Kerala
varieties.yield_notes: Unpredictable 2-15 kg/tree, highly erratic year-to-year
